title: switch user (su) command not performing as expected
body:
### Issue Description

Switch user command does not perform as expected.
A bash shell is not starting as "Login shell" and its initialization is not performed.
Respectively various setup objects (variables, aliases, functions, etc.) are missing.
As a consequence a container, which relies on "su" with login command, can't be initialized properly.
For example official IBM DB2 docker image.

The problem is observed only on MacOs with Apple silicon processor.
Any Windows, Linux or even Intel based MacOS are performing as expected.

Hardware : MacBook M4 Pro
OS : macOS Sequoia 15.5
podman : version 5.5.2 (dmg installation)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Start a RedHat container  

	podman run -it --rm --name RedHat --privileged=true --platform=linux/amd64 redhat/ubi8:8.6-943.1665521450 /bin/bash

2. In the container, as root,  execute the following commands
	useradd appuser
	su -l appuser -c "shopt -q login_shell && echo 'Login shell' || echo 'Not login shell'"


### Describe the results you received

Result:
	Not login shell


### Describe the results you expected

Expected:
   Login shell (as "-l" is specified)

comments.body: @stoyants did this work in older versions of podman?

> I can try with an older version of podman.

Great if you could try it out, but no worries. @ashley-cui @l0rd PTAL

@lsm5 OK, I gave a try with 5.2.0 and 4.9.5. Just the default setting. But none of them seems to work either.
4.9.5 even scared me a little bit, as it tried  to do something with my "/Users" directory, but there was no issue.

I simplified the test and tracked the problem a little bit further.
It is somehow "platform" related (amd64 image on arm64v8 processor)

This works as expected:

    podman run --rm -i --entrypoint '["/bin/su", "-", "-c", "echo $0"]' arm64v8/alpine

It gives "-sh" and the minus in front of "sh" shows a login shell

But this:

    podman run --rm -i --entrypoint '["/bin/su", "-", "-c", "echo $0"]' --platform=linux/amd64 amd64/alpine


gives "/bin/sh" which is not the expected result.

emulation messes with argv[0] which why various tools won't work properly. AFAIK login shells set the first char of argv[0] to '-' so if argv[0] is overwritten by the emulator it explains why the shell doesn't now it should run as login shell.

In theory the kernel has P - preserve-argv[0] flag that can be used when registering the emulators but the emulator would need to be aware of this. I think qemu-user-static supports this but I don't know if rosetta would support this as well
https://docs.kernel.org/admin-guide/binfmt-misc.html
https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/commit/6e1c0d7b951e19c53b8467e8bc4b71ee73a394ea

It is properly something we should configure for the machine, I cannot think of a reason why someone would not want argv[0] to be preserved.

@Luap99 Thanks a lot Paul

According to Apple documentation Rosetta supports the flag (since MacOS 14):

https://developer.apple.com/documentation/virtualization/running-intel-binaries-in-linux-vms-with-rosetta#Mount-the-Shared-Directory-and-Register-Rosetta

They just call it "--preserve" flag.

Now I just have to find a way to "convince" the podman to use this flag, when it creates the machine.
So far I see a single configuration parameter in ~/.config/containers/containers.conf which sets rosetta=true (or false) but no additional options, in particular setup of binfmts flags.

https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/blob/52455556919063b31ad70881fb76f79ed55cffe5/podman-image/build_common.sh#L120-L121
and https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/blob/main/podman-image/rosetta-activation.sh for rosetta

So the "patching" part is figured out already I don't think we need to expose these options to end users, as I said I see no reason not to preserve argv0.
Then we just need a test case added here to ensure it is actually working https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/blob/52455556919063b31ad70881fb76f79ed55cffe5/verify/basic_test.go#L95-L112

@Luap99 
I can't help there, Paul :(
I'm just an user of "podman", currently I have no skills to provide MR or write a test.

If anyone can provide me with instructions how can I create the podman machine or patch an existing one with the necesary "preserve" parameter, I can test does it help.

@stoyants No problem I will take care of that.

If you want to make the change to you existing VM you can just `podman machine ssh` and then edit `/usr/local/bin/rosetta-activation.sh` to add the `P` flag, for qemu you need to edit the files under `/etc/binfmt.d/` in the same way. (you need to use sudo as the files are owned by root)
Edit: Oh and you should reboot for the changes to take effect.

Also note while rosetta is the default right now we will disable it in the 5.6 image as it is broken on newer kernels so we will default to qemu at least for the next release.
https://blog.podman.io/2025/06/podman-and-apple-rosetta/

@Luap99 
Yuhuu, it is working. Thank you very much!

So, we can assume that patching  

```
/usr/local/bin/rosetta-activation.sh to add the P flag
``` 


is a valid workaround, until the fix is released.
